activity_targets.description: Deploy U-Reporters for household screening, referrals and reminders
Compile and analyse community feedback, questions and rumour trends
Select and engage U-Reporters and community mobilisers per district

activities.activity: 1.1 Hold inception meeting with UNICEF and partners
activities.sub_activity: 1.1.1 Prepare inception meeting agenda and background materials
1.1.2 Conduct inception meeting with UNICEF, MoH/UNEPI and district teams
1.1.3 Confirm coordination arrangements, reporting rhythm and implementation calendar

activities.activity: 1.2 Review ToR, district priorities, immunisation data and previous learning
activities.sub_activity: 1.2.1 Compile and review available immunisation data and district micro-plans
1.2.2 Review previous S4P learning from HCD pilot, HPV+ and polio assignments
1.2.3 Review defaulter lists, facility records and VHT knowledge where available

activities.phase: 2. Audience Segmentation and Rapid Insight Generation
activities.activity: 2.1 Refine priority audiences and geographic focus areas
activities.sub_activity: 2.1.1 Map priority sub-counties, parishes and communities per district
2.1.2 Develop household risk profiles for Ssemuju-type and Namaganda-type households
2.1.3 Conduct rapid consultations with district teams, VHTs and U-Reporters

activities.activity: 2.2 Map behavioural barriers and media/information touchpoints
activities.sub_activity: 2.2.1 Develop behavioural barrier summary for each audience segment
2.2.2 Map media consumption habits, language, trusted voices and timing per audience
2.2.3 Finalise audience and household segmentation matrix

activities.activity: 3.1 Develop campaign strategy, message framework and channel plan
activities.sub_activity: 3.1.1 Develop campaign strategy with behavioural framing and segmented messaging
3.1.2 Develop message framework across six Kakasa pillars
3.1.3 Develop media and channel plan aligned with district micro-plans
3.1.4 Develop influencer and media partner strategy
3.1.5 Develop community activation approach and U-Reporter mobilisation plan
3.1.6 Develop misinformation mitigation plan and FAQ bank
3.1.7 Develop safeguarding approach for the campaign
3.1.8 Develop monitoring and feedback plan

activities.activity: 3.2 Develop household screening and prioritisation tool
activities.sub_activity: 3.2.1 Design zero-dose household screening tool for U-Reporters and VHTs
3.2.2 Develop referral slips, reminder cards and feedback collection tools
4.1.1 Develop radio spots (30s and 60s) in English and Luganda
4.1.2 Develop radio presenter mention scripts and talk-show guides
4.1.3 Develop social media content Calendar (reels, carousels, captions)
4.1.4 Develop WhatsApp-ready shareable cards, voice notes and FAQs
4.1.5 Develop community activation materials (banners, flyers, referral cards)
4.1.6 Develop vox-pop question guides and community dialogue facilitation guides
4.2.1 Pre-test campaign messages, materials and tools with target audiences
4.2.2 Revise materials based on pre-test findings

activities.activity: 5.1 Identify, contract and onboard media partners and influencers
activities.sub_activity: 5.1.1 Identify and shortlist radio, presenters, reporters and editors
5.1.2 Identify and shortlist social media influencers and micro-influencers
5.1.3 Conduct orientation and onboarding for media personalities and influencers
5.1.4 Develop content calendar and compliance guidance for all partners

activities.activity: 6.1 Train and deploy U-Reporters and community mobilisers
activities.sub_activity: 6.1.1 Select and engage U-Reporters and community mobilisers per district
6.1.2 Train U-Reporters on immunisation basics, IPC, screening tool and referrals
6.1.3 Distribute screening tools, referral slips, reminder cards and feedback tools
6.1.4 Develop deployment plan aligned with district micro-plans and outreach schedules

activities.phase: 7. Radio Activations and Live Talk Shows
activities.activity: 7.1 Roll out radio campaign across target stations
activities.sub_activity: 7.1.1 Air radio spots across contracted stations per approved schedule
7.1.2 Facilitate radio presenter mentions and live prompts
7.1.3 Produce and air radio talk shows with experts and community voices
7.1.4 Facilitate live call-in sessions with caregiver Q&A and expert responses

activities.phase: 8. TV Personality Activations and Community Features
activities.activity: 8.1 Roll out TV campaign and community features
activities.sub_activity: 8.1.1 Air TV personality mentions, short interviews and campaign segments
8.1.2 Produce community video features highlighting caregiver stories and solutions

activities.activity: 9.1 Execute influencer and digital content strategy
activities.sub_activity: 9.1.1 Roll out influencer content per approved content calendar
9.1.2 Facilitate live sessions and expert Q&As on social media
9.1.3 Produce and publish vox-pop series episodes
9.1.4 Distribute weekly WhatsApp content packs to community actors
9.1.5 Monitor social media engagement and respond to misinformation

activities.phase: 10. Community Live Broadcasts and Feedback Loops
activities.activity: 10.1 Conduct community live broadcasts in priority locations
activities.sub_activity: 10.1.1 Plan and coordinate community live broadcast events per district
10.1.2 Facilitate moderated community dialogue during broadcasts
10.1.3 Integrate call-ins, SMS/WhatsApp, vox pops and polls during broadcasts

activities.activity: 11.1 Conduct community activations across four districts
activities.sub_activity: 11.1.1 Conduct activations in markets, churches, schools, transport hubs and trading centres
11.1.2 Engage local leaders, religious actors, landlords, market and transport leaders
11.1.3 Conduct male engagement activities through targeted channels and champions
11.1.4 Deploy U-Reporters for household screening, referrals and reminders
11.1.5 Coordinate with district health teams to align with outreach and service days

risks.description: Demand created before services are prepared to receive caregivers
risks.mitigation: Align activities with district micro-plans, outreach schedules and vaccine availability before major mobilisation

risks.description: Caregivers avoid facilities due to fear of being blamed for missed doses
risks.mitigation: Use reassuring messages: It is not too late, Your child can still start, A health worker or VHT can help you check

risks.description: Misinformation amplified during live shows, influencer sessions or community dialogues
risks.mitigation: Use trained moderators, approved FAQs, expert participation, content briefs and rapid escalation protocols

risks.description: Media or influencer content becomes inaccurate, off-message or sensational
risks.mitigation: Provide orientation, message guides, approval protocols, content review and corrective feedback

risks.description: Hard-to-reach and last-mile households remain unidentified or unengaged
risks.mitigation: Use household screening tools, U-Reporter prompts, VHT knowledge, local leaders and community actors

risks.description: Community activations attract people but do not translate into service uptake
risks.mitigation: Link each activation to referral information, outreach schedules, service points and follow-up actions

risks.description: Children or caregivers exposed through campaign documentation
risks.mitigation: Apply consent procedures, anonymise sensitive cases and review all stories before submission

risks.description: Compressed two-month timeline affects delivery quality
risks.mitigation: Use overlapping workstreams, early partner onboarding, rapid approvals and weekly coordination

risks.description: Weather, transport or local events disrupt field activities
risks.mitigation: Maintain flexible schedules, district coordination, backup dates and local mobilisation support
